```{python}

# Read in libraries
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
df = pd.read_json("https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4missing/raw/master/data-raw/flights_missing/flights_missing.json")

```

<!-- pseudo for sub-list sort -->

combine(source, destination, iBegin1, iBegin2, iEnd2)

    // The first run goes from:
    // iBegin1 up to (but not including) iBegin2
    iEnd1 <- iBegin2

    // We will fill destination starting at iBegin1
    // and ending at iEnd2 - 1
    FOR iDestination <- iBegin1 ... iEnd2

        // CONDITION EXPLANATION:
        // We take from the first run IF:
        // 1. There are still elements left in run 1
        // AND
        // 2. Either run 2 is exhausted
        //    OR the current element in run 1 is smaller
        IF (iBegin1 < iEnd1) AND 
           (iBegin2 == iEnd2 OR source[iBegin1] < source[iBegin2])

            // Copy element from first run into destination
            destination[iDestination] <- source[iBegin1]

            // Move pointer forward in run 1
            iBegin1++

        ELSE

            // Otherwise take element from second run
            destination[iDestination] <- source[iBegin2]

            // Move pointer forward in run 2
            iBegin2++

    // At this point, the two runs have been merged
    RETURN destination

sort(array)

    // Store total size of the array
    size <- array.length

    // src is the array we read from this pass
    src <- array

    // des is the array we write merged runs into
    des <- new array same size as source

    // num tracks how many sorted runs were found in a pass
    // We initialize it > 1 so the loop runs at least once
    num <- 2

    // Continue performing merge passes
    // until only one run remains (fully sorted)
    WHILE num > 1

        // Reset run counter for this pass
        num <- 0

        // Start scanning from beginning of array
        iBegin1 <- 0

        // Process entire array by detecting runs
        WHILE iBegin1 < size

            // -------- FIND FIRST SORTED RUN --------

            // Assume run has at least one element
            iEnd1 <- iBegin1 + 1

            // Extend run forward while elements
            // are still in non-decreasing order
            WHILE iEnd1 < size AND 
                  src[iEnd1 - 1] <= src[iEnd1]

                iEnd1++
            
            // At this point:
            // First run = [iBegin1, iEnd1]

            // -------- FIND SECOND SORTED RUN --------

            // Second run begins where first run ends
            iBegin2 <- iEnd1

            IF iBegin2 < size
                // If there's at least one element left,
                // start building second run
                iEnd2 <- iBegin2 + 1
            ELSE 
                // No second run exists
                iEnd2 <- iBegin2

            // Extend second run while sorted
            WHILE iEnd2 < size AND 
                  src[iEnd2 - 1] <= src[iEnd2]

                iEnd2++

            // We have now identified:
            // Run 1: [iBegin1, iEnd1)
            // Run 2: [iBegin2, iEnd2)

            // Count how many runs we encountered
            // This is how we detect when the array
            // becomes fully sorted
            num++

            // -------- MERGE THE TWO RUNS --------

            combine(src, des, iBegin1, iBegin2, iEnd2)

            // Move forward to next section of array
            iBegin1 <- iEnd2

        // Finished one full pass over the array

        // Swap roles:
        // Next pass will read from des
        // and write into src
        SWAP src, des

    // Loop exits when num == 1
    // meaning only one sorted run exists

    RETURN src
